commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex prompt/docs guidance, focused tests, prompt snapshots, a code comment, and a changelog entry to keep `sessions_spawn` deferred under `openclaw` while steering Codex subagents to native `spawn_agent`.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main defers `sessions_spawn` because only `sessions_yield` is always direct, and the PR tests pin that contract. No live Codex turn was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof or an explicit maintainer decision to accept the source-and-test evidence.

target.title: fix(codex): keep OpenClaw session spawn searchable
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep OpenClaw `sessions_spawn` searchable in Codex app-server dynamic tool payloads so Codex-native `spawn_agent` remains the primary Codex subagent surface.
- Add Codex developer guidance that routes Codex subagents to `spawn_agent`, while explicit OpenClaw or ACP delegation uses `openclaw.sessions_spawn` via dynamic tool search when needed.
- Update Codex harness docs, tests, and generated prompt snapshots for the split delegation policy.

## Verification
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts -t "turn-yield|searchable OpenClaw dynamic tools|Codex-native subagents primary"`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (first round found the prompt/tool-surface mismatch; final round clean)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Codex app-server keeps OpenClaw session spawning available through searchable dynamic tools without presenting it as the primary direct Codex subagent primitive.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout running Codex app-server dynamic-tool bridge and thread lifecycle tests.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts -t "turn-yield|searchable OpenClaw dynamic tools|Codex-native subagents primary"`
Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed: `3 passed | 221 skipped`; full autoreview-triggered focused files passed: `224 passed`.
Observed result after fix: `sessions_spawn` is deferred under the `openclaw` namespace, `sessions_yield` remains direct, and Codex developer instructions prefer native `spawn_agent` for Codex subagents.
What was not tested: No production OpenAI network turn was run; this evidence covers the local app-server payload and prompt contract before Codex receives it.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
